signals.reasoning: Cover the live MON short immediately. The position is even more underwater, liquidation distance remains tight, and the active veto regime removes any justification for defending a trade this broken.
BTC is steady, but veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH does not have enough directional clarity to justify action under the current event brake.
SOL still has some constructive structure, but the veto regime should keep it sidelined for now.
CHILLGUY remains stretched and crowded, but veto means this is a watch-only fade candidate, not an active setup.
ZEREBRO has momentum, but liquidity and trade quality are not good enough to act on during a veto window.
TAO is still the cleanest bearish name on the rest of the sheet because price remains weak and the mechanical bias still leans down, even if conviction is capped by the veto state.
ENA has trend strength but not enough structural edge to overcome the event brake.
CFX is just another momentum-by-observation name here, not a high-quality trade.
MERL lacks both liquidity quality and a strong enough edge to justify action right now.
TNSR still has some squeeze-long DNA, but the board has weakened and the active veto should keep it out of the buy bucket for now.
